gaps.title: Confidence Score Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Concurrent updates to confidence scores create inconsistent final values
gaps.scenario: Two agents simultaneously process the same ticket, updating different confidence dimensions, resulting in conflicting final scores that average incorrectly
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates two Celery workers processing the same ticket concurrently with different confidence scoring inputs. Verify the final score is consistent and doesn't depend on processing order.

gaps.title: Tenant Isolation in Guardrail Results
gaps.breaks: Guardrail policies from one tenant leak to another
gaps.scenario: Tenant A has strict PII detection rules, but when processing a ticket from Tenant B with similar PII patterns, the system incorrectly applies Tenant A's policies and blocks legitimate content
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different PII detection policies, then verifies that guardrail processing for Tenant B strictly uses Tenant B's policies and never references Tenant A's configuration.

gaps.title: Confidence Threshold Cascading Failure
gaps.breaks: Low confidence score triggers guardrail check which fails, creating infinite loop
gaps.scenario: A response gets a low confidence score (0.6), triggering the confidence-based blocking guardrail. The blocking itself gets a low confidence score, triggering another check, creating an infinite recursion that crashes the system
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a response with a confidence score just below the threshold, then verifies the guardrail pipeline handles this case without entering an infinite loop or crashing.

gaps.title: Hallucination Detection False Negative
gaps.breaks: Fabricated information passes as valid due to edge case in detection
gaps.scenario: An AI response includes completely fabricated statistics that happen to match the statistical distribution of real data, causing the hallucination detector to incorrectly validate the false information
gaps.prompt: Write a test that generates AI responses with subtly fabricated information that mimics statistical patterns of real data, then verifies the hallucination detection catches these false negatives.

gaps.title: Prompt Injection via Unicode Obfuscation
gaps.breaks: Guardrails fail to detect obfuscated prompt injection attempts
gaps.scenario: An attacker uses Unicode homoglyphs and zero-width characters to disguise a prompt injection command that bypasses the prompt injection guardrail but is still executed by the underlying model
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates various Unicode-obfuscated prompt injection attempts and verifies they are all caught by the prompt injection guardrail.

gaps.title: State Loss During Pipeline Processing
gaps.breaks: Partial pipeline processing lost on system restart
gaps.scenario: A ticket response is midway through the 5-layer guardrail pipeline when the system restarts. On recovery, the system doesn't resume from the checkpoint and processes the response from the beginning, potentially missing context or causing duplicate checks
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a system restart during various stages of the guardrail pipeline processing and verifies the system resumes correctly without losing state or duplicating work.

gaps.title: Guardrail Silent Failure
gaps.breaks: Guardrail failures are not surfaced to users or logged
gaps.scenario: The PII detection guardrail fails due to an unexpected input format, but the failure is silently caught and the response is still delivered without PII filtering, exposing sensitive customer data
gaps.prompt: Write a test that introduces various malformed inputs to each guardrail component and verifies all failures are properly logged and surfaced to users rather than being silently ignored.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Confidence Score Race Condition
What breaks: Concurrent updates to confidence scores create inconsistent final values
Real scenario: Two agents simultaneously process the same ticket, updating different confidence dimensions, resulting in conflicting final scores that average incorrectly
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates two Celery workers processing the same ticket concurrently with different confidence scoring inputs. Verify the final score is consistent and doesn't depend on processing order.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant Isolation in Guardrail Results
What breaks: Guardrail policies from one tenant leak to another
Real scenario: Tenant A has strict PII detection rules, but when processing a ticket from Tenant B with similar PII patterns, the system incorrectly applies Tenant A's policies and blocks legitimate content
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants with different PII detection policies, then verifies that guardrail processing for Tenant B strictly uses Tenant B's policies and never references Tenant A's configuration.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Confidence Threshold Cascading Failure
What breaks: Low confidence score triggers guardrail check which fails, creating infinite loop
Real scenario: A response gets a low confidence score (0.6), triggering the confidence-based blocking guardrail. The blocking itself gets a low confidence score, triggering another check, creating an infinite recursion that crashes the system
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a response with a confidence score just below the threshold, then verifies the guardrail pipeline handles this case without entering an infinite loop or crashing.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Hallucination Detection False Negative
What breaks: Fabricated information passes as valid due to edge case in detection
Real scenario: An AI response includes completely fabricated statistics that happen to match the statistical distribution of real data, causing the hallucination detector to incorrectly validate the false information
AI agent prompt: Write a test that generates AI responses with subtly fabricated information that mimics statistical patterns of real data, then verifies the hallucination detection catches these false negatives.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Prompt Injection via Unicode Obfuscation
What breaks: Guardrails fail to detect obfuscated prompt injection attempts
Real scenario: An attacker uses Unicode homoglyphs and zero-width characters to disguise a prompt injection command that bypasses the prompt injection guardrail but is still executed by the underlying model
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates various Unicode-obfuscated prompt injection attempts and verifies they are all caught by the prompt injection guardrail.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: State Loss During Pipeline Processing
What breaks: Partial pipeline processing lost on system restart
Real scenario: A ticket response is midway through the 5-layer guardrail pipeline when the system restarts. On recovery, the system doesn't resume from the checkpoint and processes the response from the beginning, potentially missing context or causing duplicate checks
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a system restart during various stages of the guardrail pipeline processing and verifies the system resumes correctly without losing state or duplicating work.

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Guardrail Silent Failure
What breaks: Guardrail failures are not surfaced to users or logged
Real scenario: The PII detection guardrail fails due to an unexpected input format, but the failure is silently caught and the response is still delivered without PII filtering, exposing sensitive customer data
AI agent prompt: Write a test that introduces various malformed inputs to each guardrail component and verifies all failures are properly logged and surfaced to users rather than being silently ignored.
